name: Canton Chicken & Snow Peas
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds chicken breasts, skinned, boned, and cut into bite-sized pieces
1/4 pound snow peas (ends and strings removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover mushrooms with warm water, let stand for 30 minutes, then drain. Cut off and discard stems; squeeze mushrooms dry, thinly slice, and set aside.
2. In a bowl, combine soy sauce, cornstarch, sherry, water, and white pepper. Add chicken pieces and toss to coat. Stir in 1 1/2 tablespoons oil and let stand for fifteen minutes to marinate.
3. Prepare cooking sauce by mixing together water, dry sherry, oyster sauce, sugar, sesame oil, and cornstarch. Set aside.
4. Place a wok or wide frying pan over high heat. When the pan is hot, add 2 tablespoons oil. Add garlic and stir once. Add chicken pieces and stir-fry until opaque (about 3 minutes). Remove chicken from the pan.
5. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the pan. When the oil is hot, add mushrooms and bamboo shoots. Stir-fry for one minute, adding a few drops of water if the pan appears dry. Add snow peas and stir-fry for 1 1/2 minutes, adding more water if the pan appears dry.
6. Return chicken to the pan. Stir in the cooking sauce and cook until the sauce bubbles and thickens.
Variations:
Cashew or Almond Chicken - First toast 1/2 cup cashews or blanched almonds in 1 tablespoon salad oil over medium-low heat until golden; remove from the pan and set aside. Stir in nuts just before serving.
